title: Majority support for global redistributive and climate policies
authors: Adrien Fabre; Thomas Douenne; Linus Mattauch

tax_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: Global tax on GHG financing a global basic income
tax_mentions.context: Respondents rate/choose support for a global GHG tax financing a global basic income; support reported and compared to the tradable-quota (cap-and-trade) alternative.

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: Global carbon budget (+2 °C) divided in tradable country shares
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: A tradable carbon budget/quota implemented via emission rights, not an explicit tax instrument.

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: a system based upon tradable permits for carbon emissions (Global Climate Scheme, GCS)
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: Described as auctioned emission permits rather than a tax rate.

inclusion_reason: Includes survey and experimental evidence on stated public support for a carbon tax: “Global tax on GHG financing a global basic income” (with redistribution), with comparisons to the permit/quota alternative.
